Problem Statement: For the following exercises, points $$P ( 1 , 2 )$$ and $$Q ( x , y )$$ are on the graph of the function $$f ( x ) = x^{2} + 1 .$$
Question:
[T] Complete the following table with the appropriate values: y -coordinate of Q , the point $$Q ( x , y ) ,$$ and the slope of the secant line passing through points P and Q . Round your answer to eight significant digits. \begin{table}[h]
\centering
\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
x & y & $$Q ( x , y )$$ & msec \\ \hline
1.1 & a. & e. & i. \\ \hline
1.01 & b. & f. & j. \\ \hline
1.001 & c. & g. & k. \\ \hline
1.0001 & d. & h. & l. \\ \hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, points $$P ( 1 , 2 )$$ and $$Q ( x , y )$$ are on the graph of the function $$f ( x ) = x^{2} + 1 .$$
Question: Use the values in the right column of the table in the preceding exercise to guess the value of the slope of the line tangent to f at $$x = 1 .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, points $$P ( 1 , 2 )$$ and $$Q ( x , y )$$ are on the graph of the function $$f ( x ) = x^{2} + 1 .$$
Question: Use the value in the preceding exercise to find the equation of the tangent line at point P . Graph $$f ( x )$$ and the tangent line.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, the position function of a ball dropped from the top of a 200-meter tall building is given by $$s ( t ) = 200 - 4.9 t^{2} ,$$ where position s is measured in meters and time t is measured in seconds. Round your answer to eight significant digits.
Question: [T] Compute the average velocity of the ball over the given time intervals. $$[ 4.99 , 5 ]$$ $$[ 5 , 5.01 ]$$ $$[ 4.999 , 5 ]$$ $$[ 5 , 5.001 ]$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, the position function of a ball dropped from the top of a 200-meter tall building is given by $$s ( t ) = 200 - 4.9 t^{2} ,$$ where position s is measured in meters and time t is measured in seconds. Round your answer to eight significant digits.
Question: Use the preceding exercise to guess the instantaneous velocity of the ball at $$t = 5$$ sec.
Answer: -49 m/sec (velocity of the ball is 49 m/sec downward)

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, consider a stone tossed into the air from ground level with an initial velocity of 15 m/sec. Its height in meters at time t seconds is $$h ( t ) = 15 t - 4.9 t^{2} .$$
Question: [T] Compute the average velocity of the stone over the given time intervals. $$[ 1 , 1.05 ]$$ $$[ 1 , 1.01 ]$$ $$[ 1 , 1.005 ]$$ $$[ 1 , 1.001 ]$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, consider a stone tossed into the air from ground level with an initial velocity of 15 m/sec. Its height in meters at time t seconds is $$h ( t ) = 15 t - 4.9 t^{2} .$$
Question: Use the preceding exercise to guess the instantaneous velocity of the stone at $$t = 1$$ sec.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, consider a rocket shot into the air that then returns to Earth. The height of the rocket in meters is given by $$h ( t ) = 600 + 78.4 t - 4.9 t^{2} ,$$ where t is measured in seconds.
Question: [T] Compute the average velocity of the rocket over the given time intervals. $$[ 9 , 9.01 ]$$ $$[ 8.99 , 9 ]$$ $$[ 9 , 9.001 ]$$ $$[ 8.999 , 9 ]$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, consider a rocket shot into the air that then returns to Earth. The height of the rocket in meters is given by $$h ( t ) = 600 + 78.4 t - 4.9 t^{2} ,$$ where t is measured in seconds.
Question: Use the preceding exercise to guess the instantaneous velocity of the rocket at $$t = 9$$ sec.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, consider an athlete running a 40-m dash. The position of the athlete is given by $$d ( t ) = \frac{t^{3}}{6} + 4 t ,$$ where d is the position in meters and t is the time elapsed, measured in seconds.
Question: [T] Compute the average velocity of the runner over the given time intervals. $$[ 1.95 , 2.05 ]$$ $$[ 1.995 , 2.005 ]$$ $$[ 1.9995 , 2.0005 ]$$ $$[ 2 , 2.00001 ]$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, consider an athlete running a 40-m dash. The position of the athlete is given by $$d ( t ) = \frac{t^{3}}{6} + 4 t ,$$ where d is the position in meters and t is the time elapsed, measured in seconds.
Question: Use the preceding exercise to guess the instantaneous velocity of the runner at $$t = 2$$ sec.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, consider the function $$f ( x ) = | x | .$$
Question: Sketch the graph of f over the interval $$[ -1 , 2 ]$$ and shade the region above the x -axis.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, consider the function $$f ( x ) = | x | .$$
Question: Use the preceding exercise to find the aproximate value of the area between the x -axis and the graph of f over the interval $$[ -1 , 2 ]$$ using rectangles. For the rectangles, use the square units, and approximate both above and below the lines. Use geometry to find the exact answer.
Answer: Under, 1 unit 2 ; over: 4 unit 2 . The exact area of the two triangles is $$\frac{1}{2} ( 1 ) ( 1 ) + \frac{1}{2} ( 2 ) ( 2 ) = 2.5 \text{units}^{2} .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, consider the function $$f ( x ) = \sqrt{1 - x^{2}} .$$ ( Hint : This is the upper half of a circle of radius 1 positioned at $$( 0 , 0 ) \text{.)}$$
Question: Sketch the graph of f over the interval $$[ -1 , 1 ] .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, consider the function $$f ( x ) = \sqrt{1 - x^{2}} .$$ ( Hint : This is the upper half of a circle of radius 1 positioned at $$( 0 , 0 ) \text{.)}$$
Question: Use the preceding exercise to find the aproximate area between the x -axis and the graph of f over the interval $$[ -1 , 1 ]$$ using rectangles. For the rectangles, use squares 0.4 by 0.4 units, and approximate both above and below the lines. Use geometry to find the exact answer.
Answer: Under, 0.96 unit 2 ; over, 1.92 unit 2 . The exact area of the semicircle with radius 1 is $$\frac{\pi ( 1 )^{2}}{2} = \frac{\pi}{2}$$ unit 2 .

Question: Sketch the graph of f over the interval $$[ -1 , 1 ] .$$
